basis.status_note: Track 2 challenge-stage work is in progress and is not part of the official score until its gates are repaired and rerun.

meaning_of_gap.score_gap10: 10 minus the current quality score for the metric.
meaning_of_gap.conformance_gap_note: Aurora is an Aurora-owned Galaga-family game, not an emulator. A true-conformance gap means the amount of better measurement, reference comparison, runtime behavior, and browser review still needed before the metric can credibly claim Galaga-like quality.

track2_working_findings_not_yet_official.finding: Challenge-stage enemy body contact spent a ship in a measured advanced-persona seed.
track2_working_findings_not_yet_official.evidence: harness:check:challenge-outcomes, advanced persona seed 3201, ship_lost cause enemy_collision at t=11.917 on stage 3 challenge
track2_working_findings_not_yet_official.implication: Challenge stages must be enforced as non-lethal bonus rounds before adding richer challenge-stage choreography.

track2_working_findings_not_yet_official.finding: Stage 4 pressure gate still found an enemy-collision loss in one repeat.
track2_working_findings_not_yet_official.evidence: harness:check:stage-pressure, stage4-five-ships seed 4201, ship_lost cause enemy_collision at t=15.25
track2_working_findings_not_yet_official.implication: Later-stage pressure should be assessed with collision-risk heatmaps and adversarial seed search, not only average loss counts.

track2_working_findings_not_yet_official.finding: Challenge-stage correspondence could write ok:false artifacts without failing the npm command when browser runs failed.
track2_working_findings_not_yet_official.implication: Evidence quality gates need hard failures and useful stderr/status summaries before long-cycle automation scales up.

metrics.label: Player movement conformance
metrics.current_read: Measured movement is at target after Track 1 repaired the recenter/input-reset measurement artifact.
metrics.remaining_conformance_gap: Low behavioral gap, moderate epistemic gap. The metric is still based on control-principles traces rather than full reference-video player x/y extraction.
automatic_assessment_course: Extract player x/y traces from promoted Galaga movement windows using frame differencing and model-assisted object labeling.
Compare tap, hold, reversal, and movement-while-firing curves against reference distributions rather than single target bands.
Run property-based input sweeps and reject changes that improve one sample while making jerk, overshoot, or shot travel worse.
automatic_evolution_course: Only tune movement constants from an optimizer when reference-derived trace gaps reappear.
Use Codex to review outlier traces and update the metric family before changing runtime movement.
metrics.effort_value_read: High value already captured by harness repair; future value comes from better reference extraction, not more manual movement tuning.

metrics.label: Shot and hit responsiveness
metrics.current_read: Close-shot and movement-fire timing are trusted, with 6 ms shot delay and strong post-shot travel.
metrics.remaining_conformance_gap: Low immediate gap, but current evidence is more about trust than reference-perfect bullet cadence and hit-flash timing.
automatic_assessment_course: Measure shot spawn latency, bullet travel, enemy-hit latency, explosion onset, and scoring text onset from captured videos.
Add visual-frame assertions for shot/hit/explosion ordering across close, mid, and edge cases.
Create randomized shot-window fuzzing for moving enemies and carried-fighter rescue targets.
automatic_evolution_course: Auto-search bullet speed, hitbox, and flash-duration parameters only against preserved timing windows and no-regression rescue tests.
Promote recurring failure cases into deterministic scenarios instead of hand-tuning from one replay.
metrics.effort_value_read: Maintain as a guardrail; new compute should mostly expand edge coverage.

metrics.label: Stage-1 opening timing fidelity
metrics.current_read: 4/4 opening metrics pass, with worst current delta 0.18, but this is still a narrow first-dive timing family.
metrics.remaining_conformance_gap: Medium. Stage opening passes core timing but does not yet compare rich rack entrance, formation-settle, first-shot pressure, and sound/visual synchronization as one coupled sequence.
automatic_assessment_course: Promote Galaga opening video into event families for rack entrance, settle, first attack, first lower-field crossing, first shot, and first kill.
Generate runtime timeline overlays and score dynamic time warping distance between reference and Aurora opening traces.
Use contact sheets to add visual checks for formation density and movement phase at key timestamps.
automatic_evolution_course: Run bounded parameter sweeps for sequence timers, first attack cooldowns, and first lower-field speed, constrained by persona safety and shot trust.
Have Codex analyze failed timeline overlays and propose new metric dimensions before editing gameplay constants.
metrics.effort_value_read: Good candidate for compute-heavy improvement because the target sequence is short and repeatable.

metrics.label: Stage-1 opening geometry fidelity
metrics.current_read: Rack target geometry is stable with no changed targets and max drift 0.
metrics.remaining_conformance_gap: Low layout gap, but visual sprite alignment is not yet separated from geometric target alignment.
automatic_assessment_course: Keep target-position drift as a hard gate.
Add sprite-crop similarity, silhouette readability, and rack-density comparison from screenshot/contact-sheet analysis.
Track HUD and reserve-ship placement during stage start so geometry coverage extends beyond enemy centers.
automatic_evolution_course: Let future graphics work change sprites/effects while geometry gates protect gameplay spacing.
Use pixel-level diff and crop similarity to steer art changes rather than asking for subjective sprite edits first.
metrics.effort_value_read: Protect the win; use it as the stable frame for Track 3 graphics measurement.

metrics.label: Dive fairness and safety
metrics.current_read: Stage-2 safety seeds pass, but Track 2 pressure evidence still shows collision deaths in later-stage/challenge contexts.
metrics.remaining_conformance_gap: Medium-low in early play, medium in later pressure. We need more distributional proof that deaths are learnable and avoidable.
automatic_assessment_course: Run larger deterministic seed grids for stages 2, 4, 5, 6, 8, and 12 with novice, advanced, and expert personas.
Build collision-risk heatmaps over player lane, attacker lane, enemy y, and recent bullet pressure.
Add adversarial seed search to find unfair collision clusters before human playtest sees them.
automatic_evolution_course: Tune dive path, steering, attack-gap, and recovery windows through optimization against risk heatmaps.
Promote each discovered unfair collision into a fixed scenario with replay artifact and pass/fail threshold.
metrics.effort_value_read: High value. This is where more compute can directly convert into fairer, more Galaga-like pressure.

metrics.label: Capture and rescue rule fidelity
metrics.current_read: 3/3 capture scenarios match with worst tracked-time drift 0.004.
metrics.remaining_conformance_gap: Low for covered branches, moderate for full state-space confidence because rescue, hostile fighter, and late-stage branches are complex.
automatic_assessment_course: Represent capture/rescue as an event grammar and validate all allowed state transitions over randomized seeds.
Extract reference timing for beam onset, capture, retreat, rescue join, dual-fighter shot, and hostile branch windows.
Add model-checking style tests for impossible states: duplicate captures, missing rescue join, lost carried fighter ownership, and score inconsistencies.
automatic_evolution_course: Use event-grammar violations to drive targeted runtime fixes, not broad capture rewrites.
Keep capture/rescue as a high-confidence guardrail while later-stage pressure and graphics evolve.
metrics.effort_value_read: Mostly guardrail value now; expand only where new gameplay work touches the state machine.

metrics.label: Challenge-stage timing fidelity
metrics.current_read: Timing metrics pass, but Track 2 found challenge-stage body contact could still spend a ship and the correspondence harness needed hard-failure repair.
metrics.remaining_conformance_gap: Medium-high. Challenge timing is acceptable, but challenge-stage identity, scoreability, non-lethality, path variety, and results presentation need richer measurement.
automatic_assessment_course: Promote challenge reference clips into object tracks for group paths, upper-field scoreable windows, mirrored readability, and clear timing.
Add rule gates for zero bullets, zero attack starts, zero ship losses, visible reserve ships, moving starfield, and readable result text.
Measure hit-rate opportunity windows by persona and by challenge movement family.
automatic_evolution_course: Use path-family parameter sweeps to maximize scoreable upper-field time and pattern readability while keeping non-lethal rules absolute.
Let Codex analyze failing path/contact sheets and add new metrics before proposing new choreography.
metrics.effort_value_read: Very high value for Track 2 because it improves both gameplay depth and the assessment platform.

metrics.label: Progression and persona depth
metrics.current_read: 20/20 persona checks pass, with one known ordering edge still missing.
metrics.remaining_conformance_gap: Medium. The game has credible progression, but not yet a full Galaga-like difficulty curve with score distribution, stage variety, and long-run pressure shape.
automatic_assessment_course: Run long-run batches over fixed seed families and compare score, stage reached, lives, deaths, bullets, attacks, captures, and challenge hit rates by persona.
Add ordering assertions for novice, advanced, and expert distributions, not only individual pass/fail runs.
Build stage-band variety reports that show which enemy families, dive shapes, and scoring opportunities appear over time.
automatic_evolution_course: Tune stage-band pressure, challenge families, and scoring bonuses with multi-objective search constrained by fairness and reference event cadence.
Use compute to find plateau stages where variety or pressure stops changing, then add focused scenarios.
metrics.effort_value_read: High value after challenge non-lethality is stable; this is the path from good demo to real arcade depth.

metrics.label: Audio identity and cue alignment
metrics.current_read: This is the weakest Aurora metric. Cue timing correspondence passes, but acoustic identity and overlap still lag.
metrics.remaining_conformance_gap: High. Audio needs better isolated cue matching, spectral comparison, envelope matching, and runtime mix assessment.
automatic_assessment_course: Build cue-level waveform, spectrogram, zero-crossing, spectral-centroid, transient, and envelope comparisons against promoted Galaga reference clips.
Separate timing score from acoustic identity score so cue alignment does not hide weak sound character.
Run isolated runtime cue synthesis captures and live-mix window captures for shot, hit, explosion, stage pulse, challenge, death, capture, and rescue.
automatic_evolution_course: Auto-sweep synth envelope, oscillator, noise, filter, gain, and duration parameters against cue-family targets.
Use Codex to inspect spectral outliers and propose new feature metrics when numeric fit contradicts listening notes.
metrics.effort_value_read: Highest score upside, but needs stronger ingestion first. Do not hand-tune by ear alone.

metrics.label: UI, shell, and graphics integrity
metrics.current_read: Shell and surface suite passes, but gameplay sprite/visual conformance is not yet its own Aurora score.
metrics.remaining_conformance_gap: Medium for gameplay visuals. Shell is strong; in-game sprite, explosion, score text, lives drawer, and attract/score surfaces need separate Galaga-family measurement.
automatic_assessment_course: Split shell integrity from gameplay visual conformance in the scorecard.
Generate contact sheets for stage 1, challenge, mid-run, late-run, explosion, capture, result, attract, score, and HUD windows.
Use image segmentation, crop similarity, OCR/text-box checks, palette clustering, and sprite-scale measurements to score visual alignment.
automatic_evolution_course: Use generated visual metrics to guide Track 3 sprite/effect/HUD changes while hard-gating rack geometry and shell stability.
Ask Codex to compare contact sheets and propose metric gaps first, then implement art/runtime changes only after the metric says what is missing.
metrics.effort_value_read: High value as Track 3 foundation, especially if the scorecard gets a dedicated gameplay-visual category.

recommended_compute_sequence: Finish Track 2 safety and harness repairs: non-lethal challenge contact, hard-failing challenge correspondence, and rerun challenge outcome/stage pressure gates.
Promote challenge-stage reference clips into path, hit-opportunity, and result-surface measurements.
Run adversarial later-stage pressure batches and build collision-risk heatmaps.
Split gameplay visual conformance from shell integrity before Track 3 begins.
Build audio cue-level comparison and optimization loops because audio remains the largest numeric gap.
Use Codex primarily to improve metric definitions, classify outliers, and generate targeted harnesses, then let compute drive candidate parameter searches.
next_step: Complete Track 2 validation before starting Track 3; use this artifact as the boundary score-and-gap map for the next long-cycle review checkpoint.
